title: General Knowledge
description: Mixed bite-sized questions designed for the public Thumbler launch and daily feed usage.

questions.promptText: How fast can the brain process a visual image?
questions.explanationText: The human visual system can register and begin processing a visual image in roughly 13 milliseconds.

questions.promptText: What concept best describes the way ant colonies solve problems together?
questions.explanationText: Ant colonies show emergent intelligence: complex group behavior arises without a central leader.

questions.promptText: Why is the sandwich traditionally named after the Earl of Sandwich?
questions.explanationText: The story says he wanted meat between bread so he could keep gambling without leaving the table.

questions.promptText: What is opportunity cost?
questions.explanationText: Opportunity cost is the value of the best alternative you give up when choosing something else.
options: The retail price of a choice

questions.promptText: Which number pattern is often linked to sunflower seed spirals?
questions.explanationText: Sunflower seed layouts are commonly associated with Fibonacci spirals and the golden ratio.

questions.promptText: What is true about the singularity in a black hole?
questions.explanationText: In current physics models, equations break down at the singularity, which signals our theory is incomplete there.

questions.promptText: Why does adding salt make ice melt more easily?
questions.explanationText: Salt lowers water's freezing point by interfering with ice crystal formation.
options: It adds heat to the ice

questions.promptText: Magnetism is fundamentally related to what?
questions.explanationText: Magnetism is a consequence of moving electric charges and the electromagnetic force.

questions.promptText: About how many human languages exist today?
questions.explanationText: Linguists usually estimate around 7,000 living languages worldwide.

questions.promptText: What phenomenon makes the daytime sky appear blue?
questions.explanationText: Rayleigh scattering causes shorter blue wavelengths to scatter more strongly through the atmosphere.

questions.promptText: Which ancient civilization built the Great Pyramid of Giza?
questions.promptText_it: Quale antica civiltà costruì la Grande Piramide di Giza?
questions.explanationText: The Great Pyramid was built by the Ancient Egyptians around 2560 BCE as a tomb for Pharaoh Khufu.
questions.explanationText_it: La Grande Piramide fu costruita dagli Antichi Egizi intorno al 2560 a.C. come tomba per il faraone Cheope.

questions.promptText: In which year did the first human land on the Moon?
questions.promptText_it: In quale anno il primo uomo è sbarcato sulla Luna?
questions.explanationText: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin landed the Apollo 11 Lunar Module on July 20, 1969.
questions.explanationText_it: Neil Armstrong e Buzz Aldrin fecero atterrare il modulo lunare dell'Apollo 11 il 20 luglio 1969.

questions.promptText: Who was the first Emperor of the Roman Empire?
questions.promptText_it: Chi fu il primo imperatore dell'Impero Romano?
questions.explanationText: Augustus (Octavian) became the first Roman Emperor in 27 BCE, ending the Roman Republic.
questions.explanationText_it: Augusto (Ottaviano) divenne il primo imperatore romano nel 27 a.C., ponendo fine alla Repubblica Romana.

questions.promptText: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.promptText_it: Qual è il fiume più lungo del mondo?
questions.explanationText: The Nile is traditionally considered the longest river, though some studies suggest the Amazon might be longer.
questions.explanationText_it: Il Nilo è tradizionalmente considerato il fiume più lungo, anche se alcuni studi suggeriscono che l'Amazzonia potrebbe essere più lunga.

questions.promptText: Which country has the largest population in the world as of 2024?
questions.promptText_it: Quale paese ha la popolazione più numerosa al mondo nel 2024?
questions.explanationText: India surpassed China as the world's most populous country in 2023.
questions.explanationText_it: L'India ha superato la Cina come paese più popoloso del mondo nel 2023.

questions.promptText: Mount Everest is located in which mountain range?
questions.promptText_it: Il monte Everest si trova in quale catena montuosa?
questions.explanationText: Mount Everest, the highest peak on Earth, is part of the Himalayas on the border of Nepal and China.
questions.explanationText_it: L'Everest, la vetta più alta della Terra, fa parte dell'Himalaya al confine tra Nepal e Cina.

questions.promptText: What does 'HTTP' stand for in web addresses?
questions.explanationText: HyperText Transfer Protocol is the foundation of data communication for the World Wide Web.
questions.explanationText_it: HyperText Transfer Protocol è la base della comunicazione dati per il World Wide Web.

questions.promptText: Which company developed the Android operating system?
questions.promptText_it: Quale azienda ha sviluppato il sistema operativo Android?
questions.explanationText: Android was founded by Android Inc. in 2003 and acquired by Google in 2005.
questions.explanationText_it: Android è stata fondata da Android Inc. nel 2003 e acquisita da Google nel 2005.

questions.promptText: What is the main function of a computer's CPU?
questions.promptText_it: Qual è la funzione principale della CPU di un computer?
questions.explanationText: The Central Processing Unit (CPU) executes instructions of a computer program, acting as the 'brain'.
questions.explanationText_it: La Central Processing Unit (CPU) esegue le istruzioni di un programma per computer, agendo come il 'cervello'.

questions.promptText: Who was the primary author of the Declaration of Independence?
questions.promptText_it: Chi fu l'autore principale della Dichiarazione di Indipendenza americana?
questions.explanationText: Thomas Jefferson drafted the Declaration of Independence in 1776.
questions.explanationText_it: Thomas Jefferson redasse la Dichiarazione di Indipendenza nel 1776.

questions.promptText: Which desert is the largest hot desert in the world?
questions.promptText_it: Quale deserto è il più grande deserto caldo del mondo?
questions.explanationText: The Sahara is the largest hot desert, covering much of North Africa.
questions.explanationText_it: Il Sahara è il più grande deserto caldo, coprendo gran parte del Nord Africa.

questions.promptText: What does 'Wi-Fi' stand for?
questions.explanationText: Actually, Wi-Fi is a trademarked phrase that doesn't officially stand for anything, though many assume it means Wireless Fidelity.
questions.explanationText_it: In realtà, Wi-Fi è un marchio registrato che non significa ufficialmente nulla, anche se molti presumono che significhi Wireless Fidelity.

questions.promptText: Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
questions.promptText_it: Chi è stata la prima donna a vincere un premio Nobel?
questions.explanationText: Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize (Physics, 1903). She is also the only person to win in two different sciences.
questions.explanationText_it: Marie Curie fu la prima donna a vincere un Nobel (Fisica, 1903). È anche l'unica persona ad aver vinto in due scienze diverse.

questions.promptText: Which is the smallest country in the world by land area?
questions.promptText_it: Qual è il paese più piccolo del mondo per superficie?
questions.explanationText: Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world, both by area and population.
questions.explanationText_it: La Città del Vaticano è il più piccolo stato indipendente del mondo, sia per superficie che per popolazione.

questions.promptText: In computer science, what is 'Binary' based on?
questions.promptText_it: In informatica, su cosa si basa il 'Binario'?
questions.explanationText: Binary is a base-2 number system that uses only two digits: 0 and 1.
questions.explanationText_it: Il binario è un sistema numerico in base 2 che utilizza solo due cifre: 0 e 1.
